title: AWeber vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare AWeber and Omnisend. See pricing, features, and which email marketing platform wins for your business needs.
h1: AWeber vs Omnisend: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: AWeber and Omnisend are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. AWeber focuses on simplicity for bloggers and solopreneurs, while Omnisend targets e-commerce businesses needing multi-channel capabilities. This comparison will help you choose the right tool based on your specific needs and use case.

comparison_table.tool_a: Autoresponders, RSS-to-email, landing pages, AMP emails
comparison_table.tool_b: Omnichannel automation, pre-built workflows, advanced segmentation

choose_a_if: You're a blogger or solopreneur seeking a simple, easy-to-use email marketing platform
You want a larger free tier with 500 contacts included
You need RSS-to-email functionality to automate content distribution
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce business and need integrated SMS and push notifications
You want pre-built automation workflows designed for online stores
You need advanced segmentation and a product picker for personalized campaigns

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your needs. AWeber excels for content creators and solopreneurs who prioritize simplicity, while Omnisend is superior for e-commerce businesses requiring multi-channel marketing. Omnisend scores higher on customer satisfaction (4.5 G2 vs 4.2), but AWeber offers a more generous free tier with 500 contacts.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, AWeber or Omnisend?
faq.a: Both platforms are similarly priced at entry level ($15 vs $16/month), but AWeber wins on the free tier with 500 subscribers compared to Omnisend's 250. For e-commerce features like SMS, Omnisend's pricing typically remains competitive relative to its additional capabilities.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to AWeber?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your contact list from Omnisend and import it into AWeber. Both platforms support standard CSV imports, making migration straightforward. However, you'll lose Omnisend-specific data like SMS history and advanced segmentation settings during the transfer.
